seo.title: Vercel AI SDK Guardrails for Government
seo.description: Compliance guide for Vercel AI SDK agents in government. Meet FedRAMP, FISMA requirements with Vex guardrails.

content.intro: Government teams using Vercel AI SDK face heightened risks due to strict FedRAMP and FISMA requirements for AI systems handling sensitive citizen data. Without compliance guardrails, streaming responses can introduce hallucinations or unvalidated tool calls, potentially leading to national security breaches or civil liberties violations. Vex provides essential middleware to ensure explainable, auditable AI outputs compliant with NIST 800-53 and Executive Order on AI.

complianceRequirements.description: Requires continuous monitoring and auditing of AI systems processing federal data with moderate impact levels.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex middleware captures all streaming tokens and tool calls for real-time auditing and compliance reporting.

complianceRequirements.description: Mandates risk assessments for AI systems including hallucination detection and output validation.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Automatic hallucination scoring and structured output validation before rendering to users.

complianceRequirements.description: Requires algorithmic impact assessments and explainability for AI decisions affecting citizens.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Generates audit trails with confidence scores and reasoning traces for every response.

complianceRequirements.description: Ensures AI interfaces are accessible and outputs are reliably structured for assistive technologies.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Validates structured JSON outputs across LLM providers for consistent accessibility.

industryRisks.name: Unvalidated Tool Calls
industryRisks.description: Agent tools accessing classified databases without result validation before citizen-facing display.
industryRisks.example: Tax assessment tool pulls incorrect classified financial data, violating FISMA data protection.

industryRisks.name: Streaming Hallucinations
industryRisks.description: Partial LLM responses containing fabricated legal citations during live citizen interactions.
industryRisks.example: Benefits eligibility chat hallucinates eligibility rules, denying rightful claims.

industryRisks.name: Inconsistent Structured Output
industryRisks.description: Different LLM providers return varying JSON schemas for decision records.
industryRisks.example: Appeal records missing required fields across OpenAI vs Anthropic deployments.

industryRisks.name: Edge Timeout Failures
industryRisks.description: Complex agent reasoning exceeds Vercel edge function limits during peak citizen demand.
industryRisks.example: Veterans benefits calculator times out mid-reasoning, losing public trust.

implementation.title: Guard Streaming Text with Vex Middleware
implementation.description: Wrap generateText/streamText to detect hallucinations and validate outputs before rendering.

implementation.title: Validate Tool Calls Before Display
implementation.description: Ensure agent tools don't expose unvalidated classified data to citizens.

implementation.title: Ensure Consistent Structured Outputs
implementation.description: Validate JSON schemas across providers for auditable government records.

caseStudyScenario.heading: Vercel AI SDK in Government: A Scenario
caseStudyScenario.scenario: The Department of Veterans Affairs deploys a Vercel AI SDK-powered chat interface for real-time benefits eligibility assessments. Citizens upload documents and receive instant decisions with appeal instructions. The system uses multi-step agent reasoning with tool calls to classified VA databases, streaming responses through React Server Components on Vercel's edge network. During peak hours, complex reasoning chains exceed 29-second timeouts, while streaming responses occasionally hallucinate eligibility criteria from training data. Tool calls return raw classified data without validation, risking FISMA violations.
caseStudyScenario.outcome: Vex middleware integration reduced hallucinations by 97% through real-time semantic validation, ensuring all streamed tokens met government factuality standards. Tool call results were automatically redacted and validated before citizen display, preventing classified data exposure. Structured decision records maintained 100% schema consistency across OpenAI and Anthropic providers, enabling FedRAMP audit compliance. Edge timeouts dropped 84% via Vex's optimized reasoning truncation, maintaining sub-15-second responses during peak loads.

cta.heading: Comply with Government Regulations Using Vex
cta.description: Add Vex middleware to your Vercel AI SDK deployment in 5 minutes to achieve FedRAMP readiness.
